questions.question: What does the '*' wildcard do when used with the ls command?
options: A) It lists all files in the current directory
B) It lists all files in the current directory and subdirectories
C) It lists only the current directory
D) It lists only files with a specific extension
questions.explanation: The '*' wildcard in the ls command lists all files in the current directory and subdirectories.

questions.question: What is the effect of using '**' with the ls command?
options: A) It lists only the current directory
B) It lists only files with a specific extension
C) It lists all files in the current directory and subdirectories
D) It does not display files in subdirectories
questions.explanation: The '**' wildcard in the ls command lists all files in the current directory and subdirectories.

questions.question: What happens when '*' is used with the ls command to list files in a subdirectory?
options: A) It lists all files in the subdirectory
B) It lists only files with a specific extension in the subdirectory
C) It does not display files in the subdirectory
D) It lists only the subdirectory
questions.explanation: The '*' wildcard in the ls command lists all files in the subdirectory.

questions.question: Can '**' be used to list files only in the current directory?
options: A) Yes, it can
B) No, it cannot
C) It depends on the ls command options
D) It depends on the directory structure
questions.explanation: The '**' wildcard in the ls command can be used to list files only in the current directory if the ls command is run without any other options.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the '.' option when removing special meanings of wild cards?
options: A) It lists all files in the current directory
B) It lists all files in the current directory and subdirectories
C) It prevents the '*' wildcard from listing subdirectories
D) It prevents the '.' wildcard from listing the current directory
questions.explanation: The '.' option is used to prevent the '*' wildcard from listing subdirectories when removing special meanings of wild cards.
